content.headline: At 68, I Finally Stopped Asking My Kids to Help With the Yard
content.subheadline: How a 2.1-lb cordless blower gave me back my independence — and why the man who built it is selling his entire stock at 55% off right now

sections.body_html:
"At 68, I still take care of my own driveway, my own patio, my own car. And I do it all without asking anyone for help."

If you're over 55 and you've ever stood in your driveway staring at a pile of wet leaves — wondering if today is the day you finally call your son-in-law — keep reading.

Because what you're about to discover changed the way thousands of homeowners think about getting older. And about staying in control of their own home.

visual_placeholder.description: A person in their late 60s, smiling with quiet pride, holding a compact handheld blower with one hand above a leaf-covered patio. Warm autumn afternoon light. Relaxed, upright posture — not straining, not struggling. Background: a well-kept suburban home with colorful fall foliage.

sections.heading: A Personal Message From Daniel, Founder of Seese
sections.body_html:
My name is Daniel. I built the Seese Pro because of my father.

Five years ago, Dad turned 72. He'd always been the kind of man who took care of everything himself — the lawn, the gutters, the driveway. He never asked for help and was proud of it. Then one October morning, I got a call.

He'd tried to start his gas blower. The pull cord snapped back hard. He lost his balance on the wet driveway. Three cracked ribs and a bruised shoulder.

The ER doctor told me it happens more than most people realize. A heavy, off-balance tool. A slippery morning. One wrong movement — and everything changes.

I spent the next three years designing something different. Something a 72-year-old man could hold in one hand without straining, powerful enough to replace every tool in the shed, and simple enough that there's nothing to pull, nothing to maintain, and nothing that could hurt you.

That became the Seese Pro.

Now here's the part I wish I didn't have to tell you.

We completed our first major production run last fall. Fifteen thousand units, stored in a warehouse in Tennessee. I was ready. Excited. Then, two months ago, I got another call — this time from my co-founder and closest friend of over two decades.

His wife had been diagnosed with stage 3 cancer. He needed to step away immediately. Our financing agreement required him to remain active in the business. The bank called the loan.

I have until the end of next month to move the entire inventory.

That's why, for a limited time, I'm offering every unit at 55% off — not as a marketing trick, but because I need to convert inventory into cash, honor what I owe, and make sure every Seese Pro ends up in the hands of someone who genuinely needs it.

I'd rather sell to people like you than watch 15,000 units sit in a warehouse I can no longer afford to keep.

visual_placeholder.description: A candid-style portrait of a man in his early 40s — the founder — standing in a warehouse or workshop, surrounded by stacked product boxes. Expression: determined but burdened. Natural side lighting. Not staged, not polished — honest and human.

sections.heading: The Tools That Were Built for a Younger Version of You
sections.body_html:
Think about the last time you used your gas blower.

You carried it out. Checked the gas. Pulled the cord — once, twice, maybe three times. Felt the weight shift into your wrist, your elbow, your lower back. And by the time you were done, you weren't just tired from the yard work. You were tired from the tool.

That's not weakness. That's physics. A standard gas blower weighs 20 to 23 pounds. It was designed in an era when nobody was thinking about a 68-year-old homeowner using it solo on a Saturday morning.

Warning: Outdoor power tools are among the leading causes of back and shoulder injuries in adults over 55. Most incidents involve repeated pull-start motions or carrying heavy equipment on uneven or wet terrain.

And here's what nobody says out loud: it's not the yard that wears you down. It's the quiet feeling that you're losing ground. That the things you used to handle without thinking are slowly, one by one, getting handed off.

The rake takes too long and destroys your joints. The corded blower turns your patio into an obstacle course. And asking the kids? That's not a solution — that's a surrender.

23 lbs
Average gas blower weight
$150+
Annual gas blower maintenance cost
25 min
To clear a 400 sq ft patio with a rake
4
Separate tools most homeowners juggle for yard cleanup

You don't need a younger body. You need a better tool.

visual_placeholder.description: A person in their mid-60s standing in a driveway, one hand on their lower back, expression of quiet fatigue. A large gas blower sits at their feet. Overcast autumn morning light. The scene should feel honest and deeply relatable — not exaggerated.

sections.heading: 950 Grams. 200 MPH. One Hand. No Compromise.
sections.body_html:
The Seese Pro weighs 950 grams. That's less than a bag of sugar.

But don't let the weight fool you. The brushless turbine motor — the same motor architecture used in professional drones and electric vehicles — generates over 200 MPH of focused airflow at 30,000 RPM.

That's enough to clear a 400 sq ft patio in under 5 minutes. Enough to dry a car completely — no water spots, no scratches — in about 3 minutes. Enough to blast ice off a windshield before your coffee is ready.

One hand. One button. No gas to mix. No cord to untangle. No pull-start that throws out your shoulder. It goes from case to full power in two seconds flat.

And it doesn't just replace your leaf blower. It replaces four tools you probably already own:

  • Your leaf blower — and its annual maintenance bills
  • Your microfiber car-drying cloth
  • Your feather duster or compressed air canister
  • Your ice scraper

One lightweight tool for every season, every surface, every task around your property. Stored in a single compact case smaller than a shoebox.

visual_placeholder.description: Side-by-side image: LEFT — a cluttered garden shed corner with a large gas blower, a rake, a drying cloth, and an ice scraper taking up significant space. RIGHT — the Seese Pro alone in its compact carry case on a clean surface. Clean, well-lit, minimal. The contrast should be immediately obvious.

sections.heading: What Happens When You Stop Fighting Your Tools
sections.body_html:
The specs tell one story. The people who use it every week tell the real one.

"I'll be honest — I was skeptical. I've had gas blowers for 30 years and didn't believe anything this small could keep up. My driveway is 60 feet long with two oak trees hanging over it. I cleared the whole thing in 8 minutes. My old blower used to take close to 20. And the next morning? My back felt nothing. I mean absolutely nothing. That hasn't happened in years."
Robert T., 71, Scottsdale, Arizona ★★★★★

The brushless motor at the heart of the Seese Pro is the same motor technology found in professional-grade drones and modern electric vehicle components — chosen specifically because it doesn't degrade over time, doesn't need oil changes, and doesn't lose power with each charge cycle.

There's no flooded engine. No spark plug to replace. No oil-to-gas ratio to remember. You charge it like your phone, press one button, and it's running at full power every single time.

"My husband thought I was wasting money on another gadget. Then I used it to dry his car after a wash — roof, hood, mirrors, all of it — in about 3 minutes. No water spots. No scratching. He asked where I'd ordered it before I'd even put it back in the case. Now he uses it more than I do."
Margaret H., 63, Boca Raton, Florida ★★★★★
Worth knowing: Every Seese Pro comes with a lifetime replacement guarantee. If anything goes wrong — ever — we replace the unit. No receipt required. No phone calls. No fine print.

visual_placeholder.description: Four small lifestyle vignettes arranged in a 2x2 grid: (1) autumn leaves being blown off a patio by a person in their 60s, relaxed posture; (2) a car being dried with the blower — no cloth in sight; (3) light snow being cleared from a car windshield on a winter morning; (4) garage shelving being dusted with the blower. Warm, natural lighting throughout. All subjects appear comfortable and in control.

sections.heading: Why I'm Practically Giving These Away
sections.body_html:
The Seese Pro retails for $249.99. That's already a fair trade when you consider it replaces four tools and comes with a warranty that never expires.

But as I explained above, I'm not in a position to hold inventory and wait for a traditional launch. Every unit sold today at $119.99 is a step toward honoring what I owe — to the bank, and to the people who believed in this product from day one.

So here's everything included in your order while this offer stands:

  • Seese Pro™ Ultra-Light Cordless Blower — $249.99 retail value
  • 3 bonus gifts — combined value of $110, included automatically at checkout
  • Free shipping direct to your door
  • 30-day money-back guarantee — test it on your patio, your driveway, your car. If it doesn't deliver, you get every dollar back. No questions.
  • Lifetime replacement warranty — if anything breaks, we replace it. Forever.

Your total today: $119.99. That's 55% off — not because we've cut corners, but because the circumstances forced my hand and I'd rather you have it than a warehouse keep it.

I won't pretend to know exactly how long this lasts. What I know is that I have a fixed number of units and a hard deadline. When the inventory is gone, there is no reorder timeline. The next production run depends entirely on how this one performs.

If you've been thinking about replacing your old blower — or about finally having a tool that doesn't punish you for using it — this is the lowest price this product will ever be.

visual_placeholder.description: Clean flat-lay product shot on a neutral light background: Seese Pro blower, battery unit, charging cable, and compact carry case arranged neatly. The 3 bonus gift items positioned alongside. Professional unboxing aesthetic — well-lit, no clutter, no distractions.

sections.body_html:
If any part of what you read today felt familiar — the heavy tools, the sore back, the quiet frustration of needing help you'd rather not ask for — then this is your moment to change that.

Try the Seese Pro for 30 days. Use it on your patio, your driveway, your car, your garage. If it doesn't change the way you work around your home, you get a full refund. No calls, no forms, no argument.

Your back has carried enough. Let the tool do the work.

Claim Your 55% Discount — Try It Risk-Free for 30 Days

Free shipping included. Lifetime warranty. 30-day money-back guarantee. Ships from warehouses in Canada, France & Germany — arrives in 5 to 12 business days.
